title: Neoalsomitra suberosa
intro: Neoalsomitra suberosa is a plant species belonging to the family Cucurbitaceae, commonly known as the caltrop or cat's claw cucumber. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Neoalsomitra suberosa include cucurbitacins, alkaloids, and flavonoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Neoalsomitra suberosa is often used to make herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are used to alleviate various health conditions, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy.
taxonomy: Neoalsomitra suberosa is classified within the plant family Cucurbitaceae, which is commonly known as the gourd or cucurbit family. This family includes a wide variety of plants such as melons, cucumbers, and squash. Within the Cucurbitaceae family, Neoalsomitra suberosa is further classified as a member of the genus Neoalsomitra. The genus Neoalsomitra is a small group of flowering plants that are native to the tropical regions of Asia.
